titlestmt.titleproper: Wu-Tang Clan party invitation and cassette tape8136

publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2022-05-09 18:48:28 UTC.

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.unittitle: Wu-Tang Clan party invitation and cassette tape
did.abstract: An early three-song cassette tape by The Wu-Tang Clan, likely distributed at the November 2, 1993 party for their debut studio album and a copy of the invitation card to the party (with Stretch Armstrong as DJ).

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: An early three-song cassette tape by The Wu-Tang Clan, likely distributed at the November 2, 1993 party for their debut studio album, Enter the Wu-Tang (36 Chambers), which would be released the following week on November 9, 1993. The impending release was celebrated with a party and showcase gig at Webster Hall (125 E. 11th St., NYC). A copy of the invitation card to the party (with Stretch Armstrong as DJ) accompanies the cassette. The tape contains the tracks: 1. Wu-Tang Clan Ain't Nuthing Ta F' Wit; 2. Clan in Da Front; 3. Wu-Tang: 7th Chamber (Part II), all of which appeared on Enter the Wu-Tang (36 Chambers). By 1995, the album was certified Platinum by the Recording Industry Association of America and had sold over 2 million copies in the US.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Wu-Tang Clan is an American hip hop group from New York City, regarded as one of the most influential hip hop groups of all time. They have released four gold and platinum studio albums.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Wu-Tang Clan party invitation and cassette tape, #8136. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Due to the fragility and potential degradation of moving image and sound recordings, viewing and listening is limited to items that have been digitized. If an item is in another media format, you may request to have the item digitized for access. Information on ordering access copies may be found on the Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections webpage.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: 2. Use of digitized audio visual materials is limited to on-site access only.

did.unittitle: Cassette tape by The Wu-Tang Clan
